title: Manual reinforcement steel bending machine
abstract: The present invention refers to a support and reference table and to a manual tool for bending steel bars. The geometrical design of the table and the stops is suitable for them to be placed at any required distance. The manual tool is designed for providing for an easier access to the bar even in cases with little space being available at the sides thereof. For manufacturing one type of pieces the stops are placed on the table in such a way that these allow making all the bends the piece requires without having to remove it from the table. After all the bending has finished, the piece is removed and the following bar is placed to proceed with the next piece.
claims: Claims 1 . Reinforcement steel manual bending machine comprising: - a support and reference table, which in turn comprises: - a perimetral supporting frame, - legs for support and elevation over the ground plane, and - a supporting gridded surface, - a plurality of stops, temporarily attachable to the gridded surface of the support table by means of fastening elements for fastening a steel bar, and - a lever for bending the bars held by the stops on the table, formed by a body of an essentially elongated geometry which in turn comprises: - a first end covered by a rough gripping surface, and - a second end, from which long projections and short projections start, facing each other for coupling the bar to be bent. 2. Manual bending machine according to claim 1 , where the support gridded surface of the table has an essentially parallelepiped geometry. 3. Manual bending machine according to claim 1 , where the fastening elements are threaded screws.
